title: Libkrun: bind mounted volumes are always read-only for container users other than `root`
body:
### Issue Description

On macOS, with `libkrun` as the machine provider, when the container user isn't root, a bind-mounted folder in a container is always read-only. Even with `--security-opt label=disable --userns=keep-id`:

```bash
podman run -ti --rm \
   -v ~/tmp/foo:/foo/ \
   --security-opt label=disable \
   --user podman \
   quay.io/podman/stable:latest \
   touch /foo/bar
touch: cannot touch '/foo/bar': Permission denied
```

The issue is reproducible regardless of whether the machine is configured in rootless or rootful mode.
The issue isn't reproducible if `applehv` is the machine provider.
The issue isn't reproducible if `--user=root`.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. `podman machine init --provider libkrun --now`
2. `mkdir -p ~/tmp/foo`
3. `podman run -ti --rm -v ~/tmp/foo:/foo/ --security-opt label=disable --userns=keep-id --user podman quay.io/podman/stable:latest touch /foo/bar`


### Describe the results you received

Files in the mounted volume cannot be edited:

```
touch: cannot touch '/foo/bar': Permission denied
```

### Describe the results you expected

It should be possible to edit files in the volume.

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

This breaks devcontainers usage: any attempt to edit a file from the IDE results in an `Unable to write file` error. See [issue](https://github.com/podman-desktop/podman-desktop/issues/13814#issuecomment-4069768331).

comments.body: Did you try the latest krunkit, see https://github.com/containers/podman/discussions/27679 https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/28308
@Luap99 thank for the links. No, I haven't. I will check this tomorrow.
Sorry just saw I posted the same link twice, I meant also the update here https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/28308
Unfortunately I am still seeing the problem with krunkit v1.2.1
I was wondering why I couldn't change my character name in Sillytavern; now I understand.
